behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and continue through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: Features: wiring. This verifies parser + hook-input handling are correctly connected for valid markdown-like content; no real process infra or model-quality judgment is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Feed multiple valid JSON stdin payloads whose string fields include code fences, backticks, and `$vars`; assert parse success and normal downstream hook-input handling without `json_parse_failed`.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: Features: wiring. The key risk is correct coupling of parse error detection and trace-record construction/context fields, which is beyond isolated pure logic but below real-infra needs.
behaviors.test_description: Provide malformed JSON payloads (e.g., missing quote/bracket, broken escape) and assert a `json_parse_failed` trace is emitted with diagnostic context pointing to malformed region.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: Features: wiring. This checks policy mapping + telemetry-intent signaling across components in local flow; no real infra required.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger parse failure in local handler and assert returned handled result, no thrown exception, and telemetry-intent flag/record set.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: Features: real_infra. Real process lifecycle, stdin handling, exit behavior, and trace emission require system-level execution.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the actual hook process, pass malformed stdin, assert non-crash behavior and presence of expected parse-failure trace signal before process exit.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: Features: real_infra, wiring. End-to-end invocation through real stdin and trace sink is primarily a system concern.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real hook entrypoint with malformed stdin and assert expected trace artifact appears in the configured real sink/output path.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: Features: wiring. This is a comparative regression across parser/trace behaviors for two input classes; it does not require real infra by default.
behaviors.test_description: Run paired cases (valid markdown-heavy JSON vs malformed JSON) and assert only malformed inputs produce parse-failure traces, while valid markdown payloads follow success path.
